Motel_management/pom.xml.general_idea: Update Maven compiler source and target properties from Java 8 to Java 17.
Motel_management/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 8 APIs, failing to utilize or enforce Java 17 language features and potentially causing runtime errors if Java 17-specific code is introduced.

Motel_management/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin version to a newer snapshot compatible with Java 17.
Motel_management/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the compiler plugin may not fully support or correctly handle the `release` flag or Java 17 bytecode generation.

Motel_management/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace explicit source/target configuration with the modern 'release' flag in maven-compiler-plugin.
Motel_management/pom.xml.why_exists: The `release` flag is the recommended way to compile for a specific Java version in modern toolchains, ensuring consistent API usage and bytecode versioning; relying on separate source/target is deprecated and less robust.
